script: Five controlled variants with natural scene voice and no visual reuse.

scenes.narration: Painting or sanding? Stop paying for cleanup time after every job.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Dynamic handheld close-up in a modern white kitchen renovation. A bearded European-American professional painter, age 35, gray T-shirt, navy bib overalls and clear safety glasses holds a dirty sheet of transparent masking film in the foreground while the protected dark stone counter behind it is clean. The dirty film has visible paint mist and sanding dust. The yellow masking tape remains permanently joined along the film edge. Photorealistic vertical commercial frame, no text, no logo.
scenes.video_prompt: The painter snaps the dirty transparent film slightly toward camera, then lowers it to reveal the clean dark stone counter behind it. Paint dust stays trapped on the film. Fast confident reveal, subtle handheld push-in, realistic film physics, no speaking.
scenes.negative_prompt: yellow tape separated from clear film, separate tape roll, blue tape, tape applied after film, scissors, knife, incorrect hands, extra fingers, warped face, product deformation, opaque film, text, logo, watermark, split screen, camera shake

scenes.narration: Press the built-in yellow tape firmly along the edge before pulling the film down.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Tight side-angle hand close-up under white kitchen wall cabinets. The same bearded painter keeps the transparent PE film folded against the roll while his thumb presses the permanently attached yellow masking tape from left to right along the cabinet underside. The yellow tape is visibly connected to the clear film. Photorealistic hands, sharp product detail, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: One thumb moves left to right and presses the yellow tape firmly against the cabinet edge. The roll and connected clear film stay fixed in place throughout; no pulling, unfolding, peeling, detaching or reversing. One continuous press-only action, tight controlled camera slide.
scenes.negative_prompt: yellow tape separated from clear film, separate tape roll, blue tape, tape applied after film, scissors, knife, incorrect hands, extra fingers, warped face, product deformation, opaque film, text, logo, watermark, split screen, camera shake

scenes.narration: Once the tape is secure, pull the connected clear film over tile and countertop.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Medium low-angle shot in the same white kitchen. The yellow tape is already fully secured beneath the cabinet. Only a narrow ten-centimeter accordion-folded strip of the connected transparent film is visible directly below the yellow edge; the backsplash and dark counter are still uncovered. The same painter grips the bottom of that compact fold with both hands at the exact start of a downward pull. No loose film on the counter, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: Starting from the compact folded strip directly under the already fixed yellow tape, both hands pull the connected clear PE film only downward in one smooth motion until it covers the tiled backsplash and reaches the countertop. Never move upward, roll up, retract, peel or detach the yellow edge. Camera follows downward.
scenes.negative_prompt: yellow tape separated from clear film, separate tape roll, blue tape, tape applied after film, scissors, knife, incorrect hands, extra fingers, warped face, product deformation, opaque film, text, logo, watermark, split screen, camera shake

scenes.narration: Spray paint and sanding dust land on the film, not the finished surfaces.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Wide three-quarter shot of the same kitchen. The cabinet edge, backsplash and counter are fully covered by integrated transparent masking film with a continuous yellow top line. The same painter uses a compact spray gun across the protected area; fine paint mist is visible landing on the film. Professional work posture, photorealistic, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: The painter sweeps the spray gun once from left to right. Fine mist and dust visibly collect on the transparent protective film while cabinet and counter remain behind the barrier. Brisk commercial movement, stable camera.
scenes.negative_prompt: yellow tape separated from clear film, separate tape roll, blue tape, tape applied after film, scissors, knife, incorrect hands, extra fingers, warped face, product deformation, opaque film, text, logo, watermark, split screen, camera shake

scenes.narration: Peel the dirty covering away, and the cleanup leaves with the film.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Close result shot in the same kitchen from the opposite side. The same painter peels the dirty integrated film inward from one end. Yellow tape and transparent film leave together, revealing a clean dark counter and tile with no visible residue. A compact roll of dirty film forms in his hands, photorealistic, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: He peels the yellow tape and dirty clear film together in one continuous sideways motion, folding the mess inward and revealing the spotless counter and tile. End on the clean surface for one beat, realistic adhesive release, no speaking.

scenes.narration: Mask the work zone in fewer steps and keep the project moving.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: High-angle wide shot in a bright open-plan apartment renovation with tall windows and a built-in sideboard. A clean-shaven dark-haired European-American painter, age 30, charcoal work shirt, tan utility trousers and clear glasses unrolls one integrated pre-taped masking film product across the work zone. Yellow tape and folded clear film are visibly joined. Energetic commercial composition, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: From the high angle, the painter crosses the sideboard edge quickly and positions the integrated roll in one efficient movement. Camera makes a restrained overhead drift, professional pace, no speaking.
scenes.negative_prompt: yellow tape separated from clear film, separate tape roll, blue tape, tape applied after film, scissors, knife, incorrect hands, extra fingers, warped face, product deformation, opaque film, text, logo, watermark, split screen, camera shake

scenes.narration: The yellow tape is already joined to the folded clear PE film.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Photographic macro side view beside an apartment window. The painter holds exactly one white-core pre-taped masking-film roll horizontally. One long straight yellow masking-tape edge runs parallel to the roll axis and is factory-bonded to the top edge of one folded transparent PE sheet hanging below it. The bonded yellow-to-clear seam fills the center of frame. Absolutely no yellow bands crossing around the roll circumference, no transverse strips, no loose tape, no second roll, no material on the counter. Soft daylight, crisp material texture, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: The painter rotates the roll a quarter turn and traces the permanent yellow-tape-to-clear-film seam with one finger. The components remain joined. Smooth short arc camera move, photorealistic product geometry.

scenes.narration: Press the yellow tape firmly before pulling the connected film down.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Oblique medium shot along a built-in sideboard edge in the open-plan apartment. The same dark-haired painter has already pressed the yellow tape along the top edge; one hand finishes the last press while the other holds the clear film ready to descend. Yellow tape is secure before film movement. No text.
scenes.video_prompt: His open palm slides once from left to right and presses the yellow strip firmly against the edge. The roll and connected film remain fixed; no peeling, detaching, lifting or pulling away. One press-only action with a restrained lateral camera track.

scenes.narration: At the far edge, tear the material cleanly by hand.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Low side view at the far end of the apartment sideboard. The connected transparent film is taut across the protected surface. The same painter anchors the yellow edge at the far side and grips the film roll to tear it by hand, with no scissors or knife present. Realistic tension and hands, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: The installed transparent covering stays taut and fixed. The painter makes one short decisive downward hand tear exactly at the roll edge; the loose roll separates while yellow tape and clear film remain integrated on the installed section. No peeling from the cabinet, no extra setup actions.

scenes.narration: After spraying, peel everything away and move straight to the next job.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Wide doorway view of the completed apartment work zone. The same painter pulls the used film away in a compact bundle while walking toward his tool case. The uncovered sideboard and window area are clean. Yellow tape exits with the clear film. Bright professional renovation atmosphere, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: He peels the used integrated covering into a compact bundle, drops it into a waste bag, picks up his tool case and steps toward the next room. Fast satisfying wrap-up, stable wide shot, no speaking.

scenes.narration: One roll combines yellow masking tape with transparent PE film.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Extreme macro product photograph on a clean workshop bench. A salt-and-pepper European-American painter, age 42, dark green work jacket and clear safety glasses holds a white-core pre-taped masking film roll under focused neutral light. Yellow masking tape and folded transparent PE film are both visible as one integrated system. No face-dominant framing, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: The product and both hands remain almost still while the camera makes a slow macro push-in along the permanently bonded yellow-tape-to-clear-film seam. No rotation, unrolling, pulling, loose strips, separation or deformation.

scenes.narration: The tape and film stay permanently connected while you position the roll.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Macro cross-angle in the workshop. The same salt-and-pepper painter pinches the product edge close to camera, showing the permanent seam where yellow tape joins clear film. His other hand supports the roll. Crisp seam detail, realistic skin and material, uncluttered background, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: His index finger travels along the permanent seam while the camera racks focus from yellow tape to clear film. The connected edge remains intact. Restrained technical demonstration.

scenes.narration: Secure the yellow edge firmly before unfolding the connected clear film.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Oblique close-up in a blue-gray kitchenette. The same expert holds exactly one integrated pre-taped masking-film roll against the floating shelf. The folded transparent film remains visibly attached immediately beneath the yellow strip while his other hand presses that same yellow strip firmly under the shelf. One continuous bonded product from roll to yellow tape to clear film; no loose packet, no separate film sheet, no separate tape roll. Product seam fully visible, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: Both thumbs move steadily along the yellow strip and press it firmly against the floating shelf edge. The roll and connected clear film stay fixed; no unfolding, pulling, peeling, lifting or detaching. One slow press-only action.

scenes.narration: Then unfold the clear film to build a barrier over the work surface.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Front medium shot of the blue-gray kitchenette. Yellow tape is already fixed under the floating shelf. The same painter begins unfolding the connected clear film downward like a transparent curtain over a pale worktop. Product seam remains visible at top, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: The painter unfolds the transparent PE film downward in two controlled pulls until it forms a complete barrier over the backsplash and worktop. Camera eases backward to show coverage, realistic folds.

scenes.keyframe_prompt: Technical result close-up at the blue-gray kitchenette. The same painter rolls the dirty transparent film inward while removing the yellow edge. Tape, film, dried paint spots and dust form one contained bundle; the pale worktop behind is clean. No text.
scenes.video_prompt: He rolls and peels the entire integrated system away in one continuous motion. The yellow edge, transparent film, paint and dust leave together. Finish on the clean pale worktop and intact shelf.

scenes.narration: On renovation jobs, I use one roll to mask faster and cleaner.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Documentary-style medium shot in a townhouse renovation with unpainted built-in shelving. A Black American professional painter, age 38, navy work jacket, gray trousers and clear safety glasses stands beside the shelving and presents one integrated pre-taped masking film roll toward camera. Confident but natural, no on-screen text, mouth closed because narration is off-camera.
scenes.video_prompt: The painter holds the integrated roll almost still at chest height and points once to the bonded yellow edge while the camera makes a slow documentary push-in. No rotation, no thrust toward camera, no product deformation, no visible speaking.

scenes.video_prompt: He moves steadily along the shelf, pressing the yellow tape with the heel of his hand. The clear film remains folded and off the surface. Mild chest-level tracking motion.

The same Black painter grips the attached roll with one hand and pinches the material at the roll edge with the other, at the exact start of a clean hand tear. No spray gun, no scissors, no loose covering, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: The installed covering remains fixed and taut while the painter makes one short decisive hand tear at the roll edge. The loose roll separates cleanly; the installed yellow tape and transparent film remain together on the cabinet. No peeling off the shelving, no spraying, no extra action.

scenes.narration: The mess peels away, and the finished surface stays clean underneath.
scenes.keyframe_prompt: Medium completed-job result shot inside the same townhouse room. The exact same Black American professional painter from the opening shot, age 38, close-cropped hair, navy work jacket, gray trousers and clear safety glasses, stands in front of fully uncovered clean finished cabinet fronts. In his left hand he holds an already removed compact crumpled bundle of transparent film covered with paint spots and dust; its attached dirty yellow tape edge is clearly visible. His right hand checks the spotless bare cabinet surface. No product roll, no applying tape, no film still attached to cabinets, no other person, no text, mouth closed.
scenes.video_prompt: He peels the integrated dirty covering off in one sweep, checks the clean cabinet finish with his free hand, and gives a brief approving nod. Yellow tape leaves with film, no residue.

scenes.narration: This dirty film carried the paint, while the vanity stayed clean.

A blond European-American professional painter, age 33, light gray work sweatshirt, black utility overalls and clear glasses holds paint-speckled transparent film close to camera; behind it, a clean white vanity and pale tile are sharply visible. Yellow tape remains attached to the dirty film edge. No text.
scenes.video_prompt: The painter moves the dirty film bundle out of frame to reveal the clean white vanity and tile in one quick depth reveal. Camera shifts focus from paint spots on film to the spotless surface, no split-screen graphics.

The same blond painter keeps the clear PE film folded and presses the connected yellow masking tape along the vanity backsplash edge. White stone, pale tile and chrome fixtures create a distinct setting, no text.
scenes.video_prompt: From overhead, he presses the yellow strip along the vanity edge from one corner to the other. The clear film stays folded until the tape is fully seated. Precise hand motion.

Tile, counter and faucet are still uncovered, no text.

scenes.video_prompt: The painter sands the trim in a short controlled pass. Fine dust drifts downward and visibly settles on the transparent film, while the bathroom surfaces remain behind the barrier.
